title: outbound_addr not supported on 2.2.1
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**outbound_addr** with **slirp4netns** should have been available for the podman version 2.2.1 per b6176d8987a0049a253fd7a70b2cd8e17bd50b53 but I am still not able to use the option on version 2.2.1

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a pod with `outbound_addr` option,
`podman pod create --network=slirp4netns:outbound_addr=ens3  --publish 51820:51820/udp --name vpn`

2. Add a container to this newly created pod,
`podman run -d  --name=wireguard --pod vpn   --cap-add net_admin   --cap-add sys_module   -e PUID=1000   -e PGID=1000   -e TZ=Europe/London   -e SERVERURL=192.168.1.34   -e SERVERPORT=51820   -e PEERS=1   -e PEERDNS=1.1.1.1   -e INTERNAL_SUBNET=10.13.13.0   -e ALLOWEDIPS=0.0.0.0/0   -v ./wireguard/config:/config   -v /lib/modules:/lib/modules   --sysctl="net.ipv4.conf.all.src_valid_mark=1" --restart unless-stopped   ghcr.io/linuxserver/wireguard`


**Describe the results you received:**
I get the following error,
```
ERRO[0000] error starting some container dependencies   
ERRO[0000] "outbound_addr not supported"                
Error: error starting some containers: internal libpod error

```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I should have been able to add the container in the pod configured with `outbound_addr`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: What version of slirp4netns do you have installed?

> What version of slirp4netns do you have installed?

@mheon My slirp4netns version is `0.4.3-1` on Ubuntu `20.04`
It looks like v1.1.0 is minimum for outbound address support.
Thanks. This was no longer an issue when using  >= `v1.1.0` for `slirp4netns`
